issue_description: CordInputStream::Skip(int count) does not validate that count is non-negative. It immediately casts count to size_t in its comparisons, so a negative value such as -1 becomes a very large unsigned value. That makes both bounds checks fail for ordinary inputs, and the function falls through to NextChunk(bytes_remaining_), effectively advancing to EOF and returning false instead of rejecting the invalid argument explicitly. This is a logic bug caused by signed-to-unsigned conversion, but the provided ASan reproducer did not trigger a memory-safety failure.
